---
title: "Georeferencia"
author: "JADM"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
packages <- c("tidyverse", "readxl", "readr", "data.table", "dplyr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "sf", "fs", "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata", "rio", "countrycode","maps", "stringi","rnaturalearthdata","devtools", "pack", "cli", "remotes", "countrycode","progress", "tidygeocoder", "dplyr", "readr", "purrr","ggplot")
# Install packages not yet installed [for the first time]
installed_packages <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages())
if (any(installed_packages == FALSE)) {
install.packages(packages[!installed_packages])
}
library(readr)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
